name: Ollie
history: The foundational trick of modern skateboarding, invented by Alan Gelfand on vert in 1977 and adapted to flatground by Rodney Mullen in 1982. It allows the skater to leap into the air without using their hands.
youtube_query: how to ollie skateboard tutorial beginner

name: Kickflip
history: Invented by Rodney Mullen in 1983, originally called the 'magic flip.' The skater flicks the board with their front foot to make it rotate along its length axis. It became the most iconic trick in street skating.

name: Heelflip
history: Invented by Rodney Mullen in 1983 alongside the kickflip. The skater flicks the board with their heel to spin it in the opposite direction of a kickflip.

name: Hardflip
history: A combination of a frontside pop shove-it and a kickflip. The board rotates in opposite directions simultaneously, making it one of the more technical flatground tricks.

name: Inward Heelflip
history: A combination of a backside pop shove-it and a heelflip. The board rotates and flips simultaneously in directions that work against each other.
youtube_query: how to inward heelflip skateboard tutorial

name: Pop Shove-it
history: An ollie combined with a 180-degree backside rotation of the board while the skater's body stays facing forward. A foundational street trick.
youtube_query: how to pop shove it skateboard tutorial

name: Frontside Pop Shove-it
history: Similar to the pop shove-it but the board rotates in the frontside direction. Often considered harder to control than the backside version.
youtube_query: how to frontside pop shove it skateboard tutorial

name: 360 Flip
history: Also known as a tre flip or 3 flip, invented by Rodney Mullen. Combines a 360-degree backside pop shove-it with a kickflip simultaneously. One of the most revered street tricks.
youtube_query: how to 360 flip tre flip skateboard tutorial

name: 360 Hardflip
history: A combination of a frontside 360 shove-it and a kickflip. Considered one of the most technical flatground flip tricks due to the opposing rotations.

name: Varial Kickflip
history: A combination of a backside pop shove-it and a kickflip performed simultaneously. A staple intermediate trick popularized in the early 1990s street skating scene.
youtube_query: how to varial kickflip skateboard tutorial

name: Varial Heelflip
history: Combines a frontside pop shove-it with a heelflip. The board rotates and flips in complementary directions.
youtube_query: how to varial heelflip skateboard tutorial

name: Double Kickflip
history: A kickflip where the board completes two full rotations before the skater catches it. Requires significant height and precise flicking technique.
youtube_query: how to double kickflip skateboard tutorial

name: Double Heelflip
history: A heelflip with two full rotations. Notoriously difficult to control due to the wide arc of the heel flick.
youtube_query: how to double heelflip skateboard tutorial

name: Triple Kickflip
history: Three full kickflip rotations before landing. Extremely rare in practice and requires very high ollies to complete.

name: Laser Flip
history: A frontside 360 shove-it combined with a heelflip. Considered one of the hardest single-board-flip tricks due to the opposing rotation directions.
youtube_query: how to laser flip skateboard tutorial

name: Dolphin Flip
history: A forward flip (frontside vertical flip) combined with a frontside pop shove-it. The board flips nose-down in a unique motion rarely seen in competitions.

name: Nollie
history: An ollie performed from the nose of the board by popping the nose and sliding the back foot up. Named as a combination of 'nose' and 'ollie.'

name: Nollie Kickflip
history: A kickflip executed from the nollie stance, where the back foot does the flicking motion. Requires rethinking body mechanics relative to a regular kickflip.
youtube_query: how to nollie kickflip skateboard tutorial

name: Nollie Heelflip
history: A heelflip from nollie stance. The front foot pops the nose while the back foot flicks the board heel-side.
youtube_query: how to nollie heelflip skateboard tutorial

name: Nollie 360 Flip
history: A 360 flip performed from nollie stance. One of the more difficult nollie tricks due to the complexity of the combined rotations.
youtube_query: how to nollie 360 flip skateboard tutorial

name: Nollie Hardflip
history: A hardflip from nollie stance, combining a nollie frontside shove-it with a nollie kickflip. An advanced nollie trick.

name: Nollie Inward Heelflip
history: An inward heelflip from nollie stance. Among the rarest nollie flip combinations.

name: Fakie Ollie
history: An ollie performed while rolling in fakie (backwards) stance. The mechanics are inverted compared to a regular ollie.
youtube_query: how to fakie ollie skateboard tutorial

name: Fakie Kickflip
history: A kickflip from fakie stance. Because the skater is rolling backward, the foot positioning and flick direction feel counterintuitive.
youtube_query: how to fakie kickflip skateboard tutorial

name: Fakie Heelflip
history: A heelflip performed while rolling fakie. The back foot executes the heel flick while the front foot pops.
youtube_query: how to fakie heelflip skateboard tutorial

name: Fakie 360 Flip
history: A 360 flip from fakie stance. Can feel more natural than a regular tre flip for some skaters due to the body rotation alignment.
youtube_query: how to fakie 360 flip skateboard tutorial

name: Fakie Hardflip
history: A hardflip executed from fakie stance. The inversion of stance creates unique challenges for body and board coordination.

name: Switch Ollie
history: An ollie performed in switch stance, meaning the skater's non-dominant foot is at the back. First popularized in the early 1990s as skating progressed.
youtube_query: how to switch ollie skateboard tutorial

name: Switch Kickflip
history: A kickflip in switch stance. Requires completely rebuilding muscle memory because the dominant foot is now at the front.
youtube_query: how to switch kickflip skateboard tutorial

name: Switch Heelflip
history: A heelflip in switch stance. One of the benchmarks for all-around technical ability in modern street skating.
youtube_query: how to switch heelflip skateboard tutorial

name: Switch 360 Flip
history: A 360 flip performed in switch stance. Considered a pinnacle of flatground technical skating and seen in the parts of elite pros.

name: Switch Hardflip
history: A hardflip in switch stance. Extremely rare in skateboarding and requires mastery of both the switch stance and the hardflip.

name: Frontside 180
history: An ollie combined with a 180-degree frontside body rotation. A foundational trick connecting flatground skating to ledge and rail tricks.

name: Backside 180
history: An ollie with a 180-degree backside body rotation. Often one of the first spin tricks skaters learn after mastering the ollie.

name: Frontside 360
history: A full 360-degree frontside body rotation combined with an ollie. Requires significant commitment and pop to complete the full rotation.

name: Backside 360
history: A full 360-degree backside body rotation with an ollie. One of the most crowd-pleasing flatground tricks when done with style.

name: Frontside 180 Kickflip
history: Combines a frontside 180 body rotation with a kickflip. Also called a 'frontside kickflip' in casual usage. A classic technical trick.
youtube_query: how to frontside 180 kickflip skateboard tutorial

name: Backside 180 Kickflip
history: Combines a backside 180 body rotation with a kickflip. A staple of intermediate and advanced street skating.
youtube_query: how to backside 180 kickflip skateboard tutorial

name: Frontside 180 Heelflip
history: A heelflip combined with a frontside 180 body rotation. Less common than its kickflip counterpart but equally technical.
youtube_query: how to frontside 180 heelflip skateboard tutorial

name: Backside 180 Heelflip
history: A heelflip with a backside 180 body rotation. Considered one of the cleaner-looking flip combinations.
youtube_query: how to backside 180 heelflip skateboard tutorial

name: Nollie Backside 180
history: A backside 180 performed from nollie stance. The body rotates backside while the nose is popped.

name: Nollie Frontside 180
history: A frontside 180 from nollie stance, creating a unique visual effect as the skater spins off the nose.

name: Fakie Frontside 180
history: A frontside 180 from fakie stance. Landing back in regular stance, it is sometimes called a 'fakie frontside half-cab.'

name: Half Cab
history: A fakie backside 180 (landing in regular). Named after Steve Caballero, who invented the full Caballerial (fakie 360). A classic foundational trick.
youtube_query: how to half cab skateboard tutorial

name: Caballerial
history: A fakie 360 ollie invented by Steve Caballero on vert in 1981. One of the most celebrated tricks of the early 1980s vert skating era.

name: Impossible
history: Invented by Rodney Mullen, the board wraps 360 degrees around the back foot vertically. Originally considered literally impossible to land, hence the name.

name: Nollie Impossible
history: An impossible performed from nollie stance, with the board wrapping around the front foot. Extremely rare in street skating.

name: Fakie Impossible
history: An impossible from fakie stance. The board wraps around the foot while rolling backward, creating a unique challenge.

name: Pressure Flip
history: A trick where the back foot applies pressure to pop the board into a frontside flip without a true ollie. Associated with the early 1990s and Daewon Song.

name: Forward Flip
history: The board flips in a forward/upward direction (nose goes down first) rather than the standard kickflip motion. A rare and stylistically unusual trick.

name: Gazelle Flip
history: A backside 540 body rotation with a kickflip. An advanced and rare trick combining extreme body rotation with a board flip.

name: Gazelle Spin
history: A backside 540-degree body rotation with a 360 backside board rotation. Originally a freestyle trick brought into street skating.

name: Hospital Flip
history: The back foot scoops forward (instead of backward) causing the board to flip in a unique motion. Also called a 'finger flip on the ground.'

name: Primo Flip
history: The board is balanced on its side (primo position) and then flipped back to normal. A freestyle-derived trick adapted into street skating.

name: Casper Flip
history: Named after Bobby 'Casper' Boyden. The board goes through a half-flip into a Casper stall position then flips back to normal. A classic freestyle trick.

name: Fingerflip
history: The rider uses their fingers to flip the board during an ollie. Originally a vert/old school trick adapted to street skating.

name: Airwalk
history: The rider kicks both feet out to the sides while airborne, as if walking in the air. Originally a vert trick popularized by Tony Hawk.

name: No Comply
history: The front foot steps off the board as the back foot pops it, then the front foot lands back on. A foundational old school trick popularized by Neil Blender.
youtube_query: how to no comply skateboard tutorial

name: No Comply 180
history: A no comply combined with a 180-degree board rotation. A stylish old-school-influenced trick popular in modern creative skating.

name: No Comply 360
history: A no comply with a full 360-degree board rotation. Requires significant timing and coordination between the stepping foot and the spinning board.

name: No Comply Kickflip
history: A no comply where the board is also kickflipped. Combines the stepping-off motion with a flip trick for a uniquely stylish result.

name: Body Varial
history: The skater rotates their body 180 degrees while the board does not rotate. Creates a switch or fakie landing without changing the board's direction.

name: Ollie North
history: After an ollie, the front foot is kicked forward off the nose of the board, then returned. A stylish old-school flatground trick.

name: Ollie South
history: After an ollie, the back foot kicks off the tail of the board while airborne, then returns. The counterpart to the Ollie North.

name: Disco Flip
history: A fakie 360 flip (or sometimes used to describe a hardflip variant). The term is used inconsistently in different regions of the skateboarding world.

name: 540 Flip
history: A kickflip combined with a 540-degree backside board rotation. An extremely difficult flatground trick rarely landed cleanly.

name: Big Flip
history: A backside 360 body rotation combined with a kickflip. The board and body rotate in the same direction, creating a flowing visual.
youtube_query: how to big flip skateboard tutorial

name: Big Spin
history: A backside 360 body rotation combined with a backside 360 board rotation (pop shove-it). A stylish intermediate-level spinning trick.
youtube_query: how to big spin skateboard tutorial

name: Frontside Big Spin
history: A frontside 360 body rotation with a frontside 360 shove-it. The frontside version of the big spin.

name: Nollie Big Spin
history: A big spin performed from nollie stance. The complexity of combining nollie with the full rotation makes this a rare trick.

name: Fakie Big Spin
history: A big spin from fakie stance. Since the skater is already rolling backward, the rotation dynamics feel different.

name: 360 Hardflip
history: A frontside 360 shove-it combined with a kickflip. The opposing rotations of board and body make this one of the most technical flatground tricks.

name: Bigspin Kickflip
history: A big spin (backside 360 body and board) combined with a kickflip. An extremely rare and technical combination trick.

name: Ghetto Bird
history: A backside 180 kickflip where the skater rotates backside and the board kickflips simultaneously. Popularized by Kareem Campbell.
youtube_query: how to ghetto bird skateboard tutorial

name: Nollie Varial Kickflip
history: A varial kickflip from nollie stance. The board shove-its backside while kickflipping from the nollie position.

name: Fakie Varial Kickflip
history: A varial kickflip from fakie stance. A reliable intermediate-level fakie trick.

name: Fakie Varial Heelflip
history: A varial heelflip from fakie stance. Combines the fakie shove-it with the heel flick for an advanced fakie combination.

name: Nollie Varial Heelflip
history: A varial heelflip from nollie stance. One of the more obscure nollie combinations.

name: 360 Inward Heelflip
history: An inward heelflip with a 360-degree body rotation added. Combines the complexity of the inward heel with a full spin.

name: Muska Flip
history: A frontside 180 kickflip performed in a smooth, stylish manner associated with Chad Muska. The term is informal and refers to his signature style.
